title: On the Economic Consequences of Mass Shootings

construct_vars: gvar_tbl <- df %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(yrms) & yrms > 0) %>% dplyr::group_by(fips) %>% dplyr::summarise(first_yr = min(yrms, na.rm=TRUE), .groups='drop')
df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(gvar_tbl, by='fips') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(!is.na(first_yr), as.numeric(first_yr), 0))

original_result.source: Table 2, Column 1 (ln_emp_pop, fips + year FEs, no controls). N=70,823.

notes: Mass shootings and local economic outcomes. 173 treated counties (2000-2013), ~2938 never-treated. Treatment = 'successful' (=1 post-shooting). PAPER EVENT STUDY (Figure 2) uses Division x Year FEs: reghdfe ln_emp_pop successful13-25 if aroundms==1, absorb(fips i.div_9_all##i.year) cluster(fips). This is Col 3 spec (beta=-1.348, SE=0.523), NOT Col 1 (beta=-2.658). Col 1 produces spurious pre-trends (t-5 coef >1.5) because Division-specific economic cycles not absorbed. ln_emp_pop = 100*log(employment per capita). aroundms filter keeps +-6 years around shooting for treated + all obs for never-treated, creating structural imbalance (treated: 8-16 years, never-treated: 24 years). CS-DID SEGFAULT FIX: att_gt segfaults on the aroundms-filtered sample (both panel=TRUE unbalanced and panel=FALSE RCS) due to 3111 fips with extreme imbalance. Fix: use balanced panel (2613 fips with all 24 years, of which 127 treated + 2486 never-treated) WITHOUT aroundms filter. TWFE/SA/Gardner still use aroundms filter (as paper specifies). CS-NT simple=-1.276, CS-NYT simple=-1.257, all consistent with TWFE=-1.348. | Outcome selection: Rule (iii.c) random selection + document. Audit confirmed.
